pattern: A palindrome is fixed by its first half, so the search reduces to finding the smallest half that makes the mirrored whole exceed the target.
hints: Every palindromic arrangement is determined by its first half plus a possible middle character, so reason about that half instead of the full string.
The first half of the answer compares against the first half of target directly; only when the halves match exactly does the mirrored tail decide the comparison.

Using half the pair budget, either build the half equal to target's first half and test the resulting palindrome, or exceed target at some half position and fill the rest ascending; keep the smallest full palindrome that beats target.

edge_inputs.description: two palindromes exist, pick the one above target
no palindromic permutation exists at all
unique odd-length palindrome that clears target
single character is its own palindrome

stress.description: small strings over a tiny alphabet so the exponential oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    t = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    return [s, t]
stress.note: small n keeps brute palindrome enumeration cheap
